claim: Humans lost body hair ~1 million years ago, likely for thermoregulation via eccrine sweating
Clothing use dates to at least 170,000 years ago based on clothing lice DNA divergence
The shame system evolved as natural selection's solution to information-triggered reputational damage
Shame at nakedness is rooted in desire for social affirmation and capacity for social self-consciousness
Feelings of shame surrounding nudity are learned, not innate
Nudity-based activities led to lasting improvements in body image and self-esteem
14% of UK adults identify as naturists or have engaged in naturist activities
Modest fashion market projected at $416.7 billion by 2025 and ~$484 billion by 2029
Global eating disorder prevalence increased from 3.5% to 7.8% between 2000 and 2018
Body dysmorphic disorder affects 1.7-2.9% of the general population
Ancient Greek nudity was associated with athletic virtue and the perfection of the gods
Colonial civilizing mission imposed Western dress codes on indigenous populations
Biopower describes state control of populations through regulation of bodies and biological processes
Engels' The Origin of the Family linked private property to patriarchal control and female subordination
Japanese onsen culture normalizes non-sexual communal nudity across generations
Hunter-gatherer societies often practiced normative nudity in warm climates
Purity culture in American Christianity emphasizes modest dress and strict gender roles
The SCCS includes 186 societies for cross-cultural comparison of norms including dress
Decent exposure laws in US vary by state; most prohibit exposure of genitals or female breasts
Evolutionary psychology faces criticism for just-so stories and lack of falsifiability
Body shame is associated with greater eating disorders symptoms
Freikörperkultur (Free Body Culture) emerged in late 19th century Germany as return to nature movement
Nordic countries have relatively relaxed attitudes toward nudity compared to the US
Muslim modest dress codes require covering of awrah; interpretation varies by culture and sect
WNBR (World Naked Bike Ride) uses nudity as political protest against car culture and body shame
Self-compassion may serve as protective factor against body shame and maladaptive eating patterns
Neural correlates of shame include anterior cingulate cortex, parahippocampal gyrus, and frontal areas
Disgust evolved as a response to offensive foods and disease avoidance; generalizes to moral violations
22% of children and adolescents worldwide show disordered eating behaviors
Modesty standards vary dramatically across cultures and generations
The INF-FNI claims to represent ~450,000 naturist members worldwide
In ancient Egypt, nudity signified lack of social status
Genesis 3 introduces nakedness shame through the Fall narrative in Abrahamic traditions
Victoria morality in 19th century England pathologized nudity and created strict dress codes
Body shaming affects 73.1% of adolescents via clothing criticism in Indonesian study
The human disgust response decreases heart rate for body-envelope violations
Modern naturist movement originated in Germany as Freikörperkultur in late 19th century
Brazilian beach culture accepts thong bikinis but not full nudity
Finland's sauna culture normalizes family nudity across generations
Body surveillance correlates positively with body shame and eating disorder symptoms
